notes: The skill achieves perfect scores (100%) on 6 of 10 evals (intent-synthesis, legacy-model-audit, scope-boundary-enforcement, retrofit, latex-verification, explain-model-logic-map), demonstrating strong IDFA methodology adherence for structural and analytical tasks.

The two evals requiring the write-recalculate-read pattern (what-if-analysis, goal-seeking-revenue-target) scored 0% with_skill due to missing output artifacts (only xlsx files produced, no transcripts or results), suggesting an executor issue rather than a skill deficiency.

Without the skill, the agent consistently fails on IDFA-specific conventions: Inp_ naming prefix (failed in 4 evals), IDFA guardrail terminology (failed in legacy-model-audit), and scope boundary enforcement (answered out-of-scope questions rather than refusing).

The scope-boundary-enforcement eval shows the starkest behavioral difference: with_skill the agent refuses the depreciation question completely (100% pass), without_skill it provides a detailed comparison table (33% pass) — confirming the skill's CLAUDE.md scope rules are effective.

Monte Carlo simulation is partially successful with_skill (67%) because LibreOffice is unavailable for recalculation, forcing a Python-based formula evaluation fallback. This is an environment constraint, not a skill design issue.
